_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD/MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma/ equivalent (in Ophthalmology)
clinical_indications: Small, red, painful lump (abscess/stye/hordeolum) growing from the base of eyelash/under the eyelid due to bacterial infection (Staphylococcus)
Watery eyes, usually affecting one eye
Painful lump on the eyelid edge

preauth.label: Clinical Photograph of the affected eye

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed clinical notes, including history, clinical signs and symptoms, physical examination/investigations and indication, available?
Clinical photo of affected part with proper labelling (R/L) and full face photograph available?

cpd_claim.q: Documents available to show appropriate post-op care and follow-up advice?
OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and outcomes of surgery?
Documentary evidence present for histopathology examination of specimen?

common_queries: Conservative methods of management not documented as tried and failed, or not indicated, for Lid Abscess Drainage
Histopathology/microbiology report missing for Lid Abscess Drainage
Affected eye not labelled R or L in the clinical photograph for Lid Abscess Drainage
Diagnosis not backed by clinical signs/symptoms/ophthalmic examination or conservative therapy not attempted/failed for Lid Abscess Drainage

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Lid Surgeries only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination and does not respond to conservative medical therapy.
